The Senate has passed a reconciliation bill using a 'current policy' baseline, which extends the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts. President Trump signed the bill into law on July 4, 2025. The bill makes the expiring rate and bracket changes and other individual provisions of the 2017 TCJA permanent.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48E) have been made mandatory. President Trump signed H.R.1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on July 4, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits and imposes specific domestic content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit (section 48E).

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that prohibit credits for taxpayers identified as specified foreign entities or foreign-influenced entities and disallow credits for projects receiving material assistance from prohibited foreign entities. These restrictions effectively require the use of domestic materials or subcomponents for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X) by introducing new criteria for eligibility based on the source of components and materials.
